All are real and fully usable. ▶There are standard weapons (sword, ax, mace, bow, daggers). but Emmy thinks those are boring. ▶Her store contains specially curated weapons such as: 15. EMMY’S SWORDS & STUFF d10WEAPONS 01Macuahuitl 02Katar 03Zhua 04Atlatl & Spears 05Urumi 06Bagh Nakh Emmy’s most prized weapons hang on wall 07A Three-Section Staff 08Tiger Fork 09Claw Hand 10Guillotine Hat (with chain) Level 3 // A goldmine for clues, whisper cards, and horror. Use them to build paranoia. [19] Cabinets: Filing cabinets tightly jammed together line the walls. ▶ Unlocked Cabinet: Old files, 3 bottles of liquor, a folded poncho and a bundle of chemical flares. ▶ Locked Cabinet: Easily broken into, it contains a medium-sized ornate wooden box. �Opening the box sends a puff of spores into the face of anyone close by - Whisper Card (p. 37). �Inside are a pair of human lungs, connected to varied growths by fungal webbing. The lungs slowly inflate and deflate. CCTVs: Each cabinet has an old CRT TV and a small clicker remote which cycles through security feeds. ▶An image of a steel door with tracks of mud below - (Clue). ▶ A Child of Ammon (p. 9). �Either a quick glimpse or a visible NPC being REPLACED - Whisper Card (p. 37). ▶ Different factions starting to rise up: �Maggie (p. 6) breaking cameras near her office. �Griffith (p. 7) gathering people into the fold, handing out food, weapons and tracksuits. Small waiting area with single wooden door leads to back room. ▶A cramped office with mud tracks throughout. ▶ Chair: An odd fuzz covers the rolling chair, its tendrils waving in the still office. Touching it triggers a Whisper Card (p. 37). ▶Old wooden desk with peeling varnish is in the center. Locked Main Drawer. ▶A loaded revolver, bullets (12). ▶Alphabetical list of mall employees & frequent patrons. Reading it grants a Whisper Card (p. 37). �Lists place of employment, birthdate and ‘viability’ from 1-10. �A select few have an odd symbol drawn next to their name (red triangle intersected by a spiral). Locked Side Drawer. ▶A packed notebook. Reading it triggers a Whisper Card (p. 37). �Runic shapes surrounding a diagram of a flesh hole. ▶A supply list: lamp, pickaxe, rope, shovel, cart. ▶Polaroids of multiple people. �Some contain the missing people. �Each picture person bound, eyes completely white, being fed a fleshy, bulbous object. �Looking at the pictures triggers a Whisper Card (p. 37). 16. THE MANAGER’S OFFICE LEVEL 3 FOOD COURT HELP DESK STAN’S PRETZELS MANAGER’S OFFICE JOE’S BURGER SPIRAL CINEMA [20] Level 3 // Kurt’s name was a very intentional choice. What could be in his bug out bag? ▶Filled with red plastic tabletops and white chairs. ▶Multiple food shops surround the space, many long since closed. 17. FOOD COURT ▶Standard three-screen cinema, with knock off concession stand. ▶Currently playing The Thing (1983), The Being (1983), The Deadly Spawn (1983). 20. SPIRAL CINEMA The windows are adorned large-scale posters line the walls, featuring extreme closeups of people eating burgers and fries. ▶Poorly locked gate. ▶The till is open, still full of cash. ▶Ketchup bottles are midway through being refilled. ▶The main container fell to the floor, splattering the walk-in door with a high-pressure burst of red. �Scratch marks line the ketchup, as though someone were dragged away. Two broken fingernails were left behind - Whisper Card (p. 37). 19. JOE’S BURGER BARN A pastel pink pretzel shop devoted to all things pretzels. ▶A picture of Kurt(p. 8) hangs in the employee of the month placard. ▶Over 100 homemade dipping sauces and toppings. ▶Kurt’s bug out bag is hidden under the front counter. 18. STAN’S PRETZEL STAND Basement and Lower Tunnels // "Abandon all hope, ye who enter here." [21] BASEMENT AND LOWER TUNNELS [22] Basement // Lots of reasons to come down here (fix electricity, clues about the mines, Mall Rats). Under the mall is a mess of hallways and forgotten storage rooms. A nightmare to navigate, even before the crisis, many of these areas were lost to time and changes in management. HALLWAYS ▶Signs of abandonment (dust and cobwebs). �Closer inspection reveals an inordinate amount of heavy traffic (scuffs, dirt, things pushed to clear more space, dark stains). �All the tracks lead to the Bunker (p. 23). UNMARKED STORAGE ROOMS ▶Large, full of old mall furniture and fixtures. ▶Connects the Access Hallways (p. 11) to each floor, including the basement. ▶Florescent light flickers incessantly. ▶Specks of blood, mud, and mold stain the floor of the elevator. ▶Some bloody fingernails are embedded in the tattered padding along the walls. 21A. FREIGHT ELEVATOR Failing lights that fade the closer toward the basement you go. ▶Oppressive concrete with graffiti filling the stairwell near the basement. ▶There is vent access sporadically throughout. ▶Double doors open into the basement. These frequently jam and require effort to open. 21B. MAINTENANCE STAIRWELL Right off the basement’s main entrance, a pair of large steel doors lead to the backup generators and water pumps. ▶Two massive generators fill the main room, with utility cables webbing out and up. ▶Large chrome fuel tanks line the back wall, tubes connecting them to the generators. ▶The connecting room pumps water from an underground well, all the way up to rooftop water towers. 22. UTILITY ROOM Located in a long-abandoned storeroom (with improvised locking mechanism making entrance from the basement hallways difficult). ▶Old TV and game system, surrounded by ad-hoc furniture. ▶There is vent access from inside. This is the main way in and out of the clubhouse. ▶The higher the DOOM Level, the more high-quality supplies are being stored here. ▶50% chance a wounded Mall Rat is being tended to by their friend. A large amount of blood pools around them. 23. MALL RATS CLUBHOUSE BASEMENT The Bunker & Below // Here’s where things become most dungeon-like. Survival is far from guaranteed. [23] THE BUNKER AND
